title: Nginx Returning 502 Bad Gateway Due to Upstream Socket Exhaustion
symptoms: Intermittent 502 Bad Gateway errors from nginx-prod-web-02
Nginx error log showing 'connect() failed: Cannot assign requested address'
Ephemeral port range exhausted (all 28,000 ports in TIME_WAIT)
Upstream servers healthy and responding normally to direct requests
Connection count on nginx node at kernel maximum
root_cause: Nginx keepalive configuration set to 0, disabling connection reuse to upstream servers, causing ephemeral port exhaustion during traffic spikes
resolution: Corrected nginx keepalive configuration to maintain 256 persistent connections per upstream, restarted nginx to release TIME_WAIT sockets, and increased ephemeral port range
preventive_actions: Audit nginx keepalive configuration across all proxy nodes
Add ephemeral port utilization monitoring with alerts
Implement nginx config validation in deployment pipeline
Create performance testing baseline for nginx connection handling
Add connection pool metrics to the SRE dashboard

timeline.event: Error rate alert triggers, Alex Johnson begins investigation
Upstream servers confirmed healthy, issue isolated to nginx layer
Ephemeral port exhaustion identified from kernel TCP metrics
TIME_WAIT connections draining, error rate declining
Error rate at 0%, all traffic flowing normally

embedding_summary: Nginx 502 Bad Gateway errors caused by ephemeral port exhaustion due to keepalive misconfiguration disabling connection reuse to upstream servers. 30% of web traffic affected for 20 minutes. Resolved by enabling keepalive connections and expanding ephemeral port range.
